docs.title: Squirrel
docs.text: Welcome to the Squirrel user guide. Squirrel application for configuration management of EPICS Process Variables (PVs).

docs.title: What is Squirrel?
docs.text:
Squirrel provides a responsive interface for:

  • Creating snapshots of control system states
  • Comparing configurations between different points in time
  • Restoring values to return systems to known states

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Description Snapshot Management Create, view, and delete snapshots of PV values Live PV Values Real-time display of current values alongside saved values Snapshot Comparison Side-by-side comparison with difference highlighting Restore Functionality Preview and restore saved setpoint values

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: New to Squirrel? Start with the Getting Started guide to learn the basics.

docs.title: User Guide
docs.text: For detailed instructions on each feature, see the User Guide.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will help you get up and running with Squirrel.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before using Squirrel, ensure:

  1. The backend API (squirrel backend) is running and accessible
  2. You have a modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, or Safari)
  3. Network access to your EPICS control system (for live values)

docs.title: Accessing the Application
docs.text: Open your web browser and navigate to the Squirrel URL provided by your administrator.

docs.title: User Interface Overview
docs.text: Squirrel has a simple, consistent layout:

docs.title: Sidebar Navigation
docs.text:
The left sidebar provides access to all main features:

  • Snapshots - View and manage saved snapshots
  • PV Browser - Browse and search for PVs
  • Tags - Manage tag groups for organizing PVs

docs.title: Main Content Area
docs.text:
The main area displays the content for the selected feature. Most views include:

  • Search bar - Filter items by name or other criteria
  • Action buttons - Create, delete, or perform operations
  • Data table - View and interact with data

docs.title: Admin Mode
docs.text: Some features require admin privileges. If you have admin access, you can toggle admin mode using the switch in the sidebar.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Learn about Snapshots - the core feature for saving PV states
  • Explore the PV Browser to find and manage PVs
  • Set up Tags to organize your PVs

docs.title: User Guide
docs.text: This guide covers all the features of Squirrel in detail.

docs.title: Saving and Restoring Configurations
docs.text:
1. Snapshots - Create snapshots to save the current state of your PVs
  2. Restore - Restore saved values back to the control system

docs.title: Comparing Configurations
docs.text: 1. Snapshot Comparison - Compare two snapshots to see what changed

docs.title: Managing PVs
docs.text:
1. PV Browser - Find, add, and import PVs
  2. PV Details - View detailed information about individual PVs
  3. Tags - Organize PVs into groups for easier filtering

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: Task Where to Go Save current PV values Snapshots → Create Snapshot View a saved snapshot Snapshots → Click snapshot name Compare two snapshots Snapshot Details → Compare button Find a specific PV PV Browser → Search Add new PVs PV Browser → Add PV or Import CSV Restore saved values Snapshot Details → Select PVs → Restore Filter PVs by tag Any PV table → Tag filter dropdown

docs.title: PV Browser
docs.text: The PV Browser lets you explore, search, and manage the Process Variables (PVs) in your system.

docs.title: Accessing the PV Browser
docs.text: Click PV Browser in the sidebar to open the browser view.

docs.title: Browsing PVs
docs.text:
The PV Browser displays all configured PVs in a table:

Column Description PV Name The full process variable name Device The device this PV belongs to Description A human-readable description Live Value Current real-time value Tags Assigned tag groups

docs.title: Searching for PVs
docs.text: Use the search bar to find specific PVs.

docs.title: Wildcard Search
docs.text:
You can use wildcards to search for patterns:

  • * matches any characters
  • ? matches a single character

Examples:

Search Pattern Matches QUAD:* All PVs starting with "QUAD:" *:BDES All PVs ending with ":BDES" QUAD:*:BDES BDES PVs for all QUADs

docs.title: Adding PVs
docs.text:
Admin Required

Adding PVs requires admin privileges.

docs.title: Adding Individual PVs
docs.text:
1. Click the Add PV button
  2. Enter the PV name
  3. Fill in optional details (device, description)
  4. Click Add

docs.title: Importing from CSV
docs.text:
For bulk additions, you can import PVs from a CSV file.

  1. Click the Import CSV button
  2. Select your CSV file
  3. Review the import preview
  4. Click Import

docs.title: CSV Format
docs.text:
The CSV file should have the following columns:

pv_name,device,description
QUAD:001:BDES,QUAD:001,Quad 1 BDES setpoint
QUAD:001:BACT,QUAD:001,Quad 1 BACT readback

CSV Headers

The first row should contain column headers. At minimum, pv_name is required.

docs.title: Viewing PV Details
docs.text: Click on a PV name to open its details page.

docs.title: Managing PV Tags
docs.text:
You can assign tags to PVs to organize them into groups:

  1. Select one or more PVs using checkboxes
  2. Click the Tags button
  3. Select the tags to assign
  4. Click Save

See Tags for more information on tag management.

docs.title: PV Details
docs.text: The PV Details page shows comprehensive information about a single Process Variable.

docs.title: Accessing PV Details
docs.text: Click on any PV name in a table to open its details page.

docs.title: Basic Information
docs.text: Field Description PV Name The full process variable name Device The device this PV belongs to Description Human-readable description of the PV

docs.title: Current Values
docs.text:
Field Description Setpoint The current setpoint value Readback The current readback value Config Value The configured/nominal value

Values update in real-time when connected to the control system.

docs.title: Assigned Tags
docs.text: The details page shows all tag groups this PV belongs to. Tags help organize PVs for filtering in other views.

docs.title: Editing PV Information
docs.text:
Admin Required

Editing PV information requires admin privileges.

With admin mode enabled:

  1. Click the Edit button
  2. Modify the fields as needed
  3. Click Save

Editable fields include:

  • Device
  • Description
  • Tag assignments

PV Name

The PV name itself cannot be changed after creation.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
From the PV Details page, you can:

  • Click Back to return to the previous view
  • Use the sidebar to navigate to other sections

docs.title: Restore
docs.text: The restore feature allows you to apply saved setpoint values from a snapshot back to the control system.

docs.title: When to Use Restore
docs.text:
Common use cases:

  • Recovery - Return to a known good state after an issue
  • Maintenance - Restore settings after maintenance work
  • Testing - Reset to baseline between tests
  • Rollback - Undo recent changes

docs.title: Starting a Restore
docs.text:
1. Open a snapshot's details page
  2. Select the PVs you want to restore using checkboxes
  3. Click the Restore button

docs.title: Preview Dialog
docs.text:
Before applying values, a preview dialog shows exactly what will be restored.

The preview shows:

Column Description PV Name The process variable to be restored Saved Setpoint The value that will be applied Current Value The current live value (for reference)

Review Carefully

Always review the preview before confirming. Restored values are applied immediately to the control system.

docs.title: Confirming the Restore
docs.text:
1. Review the PVs and values in the preview
  2. Click Restore to apply the values
  3. Wait for confirmation

The restore operation sends the saved setpoint values to the control system.

docs.title: Select All
docs.text: Use the checkbox in the header row to select all PVs.

docs.title: Select Individual PVs
docs.text: Click the checkbox next to each PV you want to include.

docs.title: Filter Then Select
docs.text:
1. Use search or tag filters to narrow down the list
  2. Select the PVs you need
  3. Proceed with restore

Partial Restore

You don't have to restore all PVs. Select only the ones you need to change.

docs.title: Safety Considerations
docs.text:
Production Systems

Restoring values affects the live control system. Ensure you have appropriate authorization before performing restores on production systems.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Verify the snapshot - Make sure you're restoring from the correct snapshot
  2. Review the preview - Check that the values look correct
  3. Start small - When uncertain, restore a few PVs first to verify behavior
  4. Communicate - Inform relevant operators before restoring values
  5. Document - Note why the restore was performed

docs.title: Restore Failed
docs.text:
If a restore fails:

  • Check network connectivity to the control system
  • Verify the PVs are accessible
  • Check for write permission issues
  • Contact your system administrator

docs.title: Partial Success
docs.text: Some PVs may fail while others succeed. The system will report which PVs could not be restored.

docs.title: Snapshot Comparison
docs.text: The comparison view lets you see differences between two snapshots side by side, making it easy to identify what changed between configurations.

docs.title: Starting a Comparison
docs.text: There are two ways to start a comparison:

docs.title: From Snapshot Details
docs.text:
1. Open a snapshot's details page
  2. Click the Compare button
  3. Select the second snapshot to compare against

docs.title: From the Snapshot List
docs.text:
1. Select two snapshots using the checkboxes
  2. Click the Compare button

docs.title: Understanding the Comparison View
docs.text:
The comparison displays PVs in a table with columns from both snapshots:

Column Description PV Name The process variable name Main Setpoint Setpoint value from the first (main) snapshot Main Readback Readback value from the first snapshot Compare Setpoint Setpoint value from the second snapshot Compare Readback Readback value from the second snapshot

docs.title: Color-Coded Differences
docs.text:
The comparison view highlights differences to make them easy to spot:

  • Highlighted rows - PVs where values differ between snapshots
  • Normal rows - PVs with identical values in both snapshots

Finding Changes Quickly

Use the filter options to show only PVs with differences, hiding identical values.

docs.title: Filtering the Comparison
docs.text:
Use the controls at the top to focus on specific PVs:

  • Search - Filter by PV name
  • Show differences only - Hide PVs with identical values
  • Tag filter - Show only PVs with specific tags

docs.title: Performance
docs.text: The comparison view uses virtual scrolling to handle large numbers of PVs efficiently. Even with 10,000+ PVs, scrolling remains smooth.

docs.title: Before/After Maintenance
docs.text: Compare a "before maintenance" snapshot with an "after maintenance" snapshot to verify only intended changes were made.

docs.title: Configuration Drift Detection
docs.text: Compare the current state against a baseline configuration to identify unexpected changes.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Compare a "working" snapshot with the current state to identify what changed before a problem occurred.

docs.title: Snapshots
docs.text: Snapshots are the core feature of Squirrel. A snapshot captures the values of all configured PVs at a specific point in time, allowing you to save, compare, and restore control system states.

docs.title: What is a Snapshot?
docs.text:
A snapshot contains:

  • Timestamp - When the snapshot was created
  • Comment - A description of the snapshot purpose
  • PV Values - The setpoint and readback values for each PV at capture time

docs.title: Viewing the Snapshot List
docs.text:
Navigate to Snapshots in the sidebar to see all saved snapshots.

The list shows:

  • Snapshot name/comment
  • Creation date and time
  • Number of PVs captured

Use the search bar to filter snapshots by name.

docs.title: Creating a Snapshot
docs.text:
1. Click the Create Snapshot button
  2. Enter a descriptive comment for the snapshot
  3. Click Create

Naming Conventions

Use descriptive comments that indicate the purpose or state, such as "Before maintenance" or "Baseline configuration 2024-01".

The snapshot will capture the current values of all configured PVs. A progress indicator shows the capture status.

docs.title: Viewing Snapshot Details
docs.text:
Click on a snapshot name to view its details.

The details page shows:

Column Description PV Name The process variable name Saved Setpoint The setpoint value when the snapshot was taken Saved Readback The readback value when the snapshot was taken Live Value The current real-time value (updates automatically) Severity Alarm severity indicator

docs.title: Filtering PVs
docs.text:
Use the controls at the top to filter the PV list:

  • Search - Filter by PV name or device
  • Tag filter - Show only PVs with specific tags

docs.title: Selecting PVs
docs.text:
Click the checkbox next to PVs to select them for:

  • Restore - Apply saved values back to the control system
  • Compare - Include in snapshot comparison

docs.title: Deleting a Snapshot
docs.text:
Admin Required

Deleting snapshots requires admin privileges.

  1. Select the snapshot(s) to delete
  2. Click the Delete button
  3. Confirm the deletion

Deleted snapshots cannot be recovered.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Compare two snapshots to see differences
  • Restore values from a snapshot to the control system

docs.title: Tags
docs.text: Tags help you organize PVs into logical groups for easier filtering and management.

docs.title: What are Tags?
docs.text:
Tags are labels you can assign to PVs. A PV can have multiple tags, and tags can be organized into tag groups.

Examples of tag groups:

  • System - Magnets, RF, Vacuum, Diagnostics
  • Location - Sector 1, Sector 2, Injector, Linac
  • Priority - Critical, Standard, Optional

docs.title: Accessing Tag Management
docs.text: Click Tags in the sidebar to manage tag groups.

docs.title: Filtering by Tags
docs.text:
Throughout Squirrel, you can filter PV tables by tags:

  1. Look for the tag filter dropdown above the table
  2. Select one or more tags
  3. The table updates to show only matching PVs

This works in:

  • Snapshot Details
  • Snapshot Comparison
  • PV Browser

docs.title: Managing Tag Groups
docs.text:
Admin Required

Creating and modifying tag groups requires admin privileges.

docs.title: Creating a Tag Group
docs.text:
1. Click Create Tag Group
  2. Enter a name for the group
  3. Add tags within the group
  4. Click Save

docs.title: Editing a Tag Group
docs.text:
1. Click on the tag group name
  2. Modify the name or tags
  3. Click Save

docs.title: Adding Tags to a Group
docs.text:
1. Open the tag group
  2. Click Add Tag
  3. Enter the tag name
  4. Click Add

docs.title: Deleting Tags
docs.text:
1. Open the tag group
  2. Click the delete icon next to the tag
  3. Confirm the deletion

Impact of Deleting Tags

Deleting a tag removes it from all PVs that have it assigned.

docs.title: Assigning Tags to PVs
docs.text: You can assign tags to PVs from:

docs.title: PV Browser
docs.text:
1. Select PVs using checkboxes
  2. Click Tags
  3. Select tags to assign
  4. Click Save

docs.title: PV Details
docs.text:
1. Open the PV details page
  2. Click Edit (admin mode required)
  3. Modify tag assignments
  4. Click Save

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text: !!! tip "Tag Organization" - Use consistent naming conventions - Create tag groups that match your organizational structure - Avoid too many tags - keep it manageable - Use descriptive names that are clear to all users
